title: PROJECT ENGINEER - FIRE PROTECTION
description:
Job Purpose:

The Project Engineer (Fire Sprinkler System / Mechanical) is responsible for managing and executing fire protection system projects, specifically sprinkler systems. This includes project planning, material and design coordination, site supervision, testing, commissioning, and ensuring compliance with statutory requirements and international standards.

________________________________________

Key Responsibilities:

1. Project Planning & Coordination

• Plan and schedule project activities in line with approved timelines.

• Prepare and submit technical drawings, hydraulic calculations, and material submittals for consultant and authority approval.

• Coordinate with clients, consultants, contractors, and suppliers for smooth project execution.

2. Installation & Supervision

• Supervise the installation of fire sprinkler systems, piping, pumps, and related equipment.

• Ensure site works comply with design, NFPA standards, and local authority requirements.

• Conduct site inspections and resolve technical/site issues promptly.

3. Testing & Commissioning

• Carry out system testing and commissioning, including hydrostatic pressure testing, flow testing, and pump performance tests.

• Coordinate with authorities (e.g., Fire Department) for inspections and final approvals.

4. Documentation & Reporting

• Prepare project progress reports, method statements, and risk assessments.

• Maintain proper documentation of drawings, approvals, and test certificates.

• Monitor project costs, materials, and manpower to ensure efficiency.

5. Compliance & Quality

• Ensure compliance with fire safety regulations, building codes, and company standards.

• Enforce workplace safety and quality assurance procedures on-site.

________________________________________

Requirements:

• Degree/Diploma in Mechanical Engineering or related field.

• Minimum 2–3 years’ experience in fire protection or M&E projects (fresh graduates may be considered for junior roles).

• Strong knowledge of fire sprinkler systems, pumps, valves, piping, and related equipment.

• Familiarity with NFPA, and local fire authority standards/regulations.

• Excellent coordination, problem-solving, and communication skills.

• Willing to work at project sites and handle multiple projects.

________________________________________

Preferred Skills (Added Advantage):

• Experience in AutoCAD, Revit/BIM, or similar design/drafting software.

• Hands-on knowledge of fire pumps and sprinkler system commissioning.

• Registration with relevant local authority or professional body.

postedCompany.description: A progressive privately owned independent company operating successfully since 1983. We are the only privately owned specialist Fire Protection Contractor registered with the Building and Construction Authority, pre- qualified to tender all government projects with an unlimited financial limit. We have also expanded our business to Thailand, Vietnam, Malaysia and Myanmar to cater to our overseas markets.
postedCompany.name: DELUGE FIRE PROTECTION (S.E.A.) PTE LTD
